meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How Hargrove Wealth Advisory collects, uses, protects, and never sells your personal information.

intro: Hargrove Wealth Advisory ("we," "our," or "us") respects your privacy and is committed to protecting it. As a registered investment adviser, we are also bound by federal and state privacy regulations that apply specifically to financial services firms. This policy describes what information we collect, how we use it, and the controls you have over it.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
sections.body:
We collect information you provide to us directly — when you fill out our contact form, schedule a consultation, become a client, or correspond with us by phone or email. This may include your name, address, phone number, email address, date of birth, Social Security number, employment information, financial account information, investment objectives, and other details necessary to provide our services.

We also collect limited information automatically when you visit our website, including basic analytics data such as the pages you visited, your approximate location based on IP address, the device and browser you used, and the date and time of your visit. We do not use this information to identify you personally.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Information
sections.body: We use the information you provide to deliver our advisory services: building your financial plan, managing your investment accounts, preparing reports, responding to your questions, meeting regulatory and tax-reporting obligations, and communicating with you about your account and our firm. We do not use your information for any purpose unrelated to providing you with financial advisory services.

sections.heading: Data Sharing and Third Parties
sections.body:
**We do not sell your personal information.** Ever. We do not rent it, trade it, or share it with marketers. Period.

We do share information with a limited set of service providers who are necessary to deliver our services — such as the custodian that holds your accounts (e.g. Schwab or Fidelity), our portfolio reporting and financial planning software providers, our CPA partners (only with your authorization), and our internal accounting and compliance systems. Every third party we use is bound by confidentiality requirements and is permitted to use your information only for the purpose of supporting our services.

We may also disclose information when required by law — for example, in response to a valid subpoena, court order, or regulatory examination — and we will notify you of such a disclosure where we are legally permitted to do so.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: We maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect your information. These include encrypted storage of sensitive data, role-based access controls, multi-factor authentication for all employees, secure document portals for exchanging financial information (we strongly discourage clients from sending account numbers or tax documents over standard email), and annual security training for all staff. No system is perfectly secure, but we treat your information with the seriousness a financial firm should.

sections.heading: Your Rights
Cookies and Website Analytics
sections.body: Our website uses minimal first-party cookies necessary for the site to function. We use a privacy-respecting analytics tool that does not track you across other sites and does not require a cookie banner under most regulations. You can block all cookies in your browser settings without losing functionality on our site.

sections.heading: Children's Privacy
sections.body: Our services are not directed to children under 18, and we do not knowingly collect information from anyone under 18.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.body: We may update this policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or regulatory requirements. When we do, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page and, for material changes, notify active clients directly.

sections.heading: Contact for Privacy Matters
sections.body: If you have a question, concern, or request related to your privacy, please reach out:
